creator.name: Yeigh, Frank
roles: charter member Alpine Club of Canada

archival_fonds.title: Frank Yeigh fonds
contents: scrapbooks with historical clippings on Ontario topics
correspondence as Chairman Historical Committee Canadian Club Toronto
archival_fonds.provenance: Donated by Annie Louise Laird Yeigh (wife), March 1959

archival_fonds.title: Frank Yeigh fonds - Pauline Johnson materials
contents: Johnson poems including 'If I Could Only Know'
Materials relating to Pauline Johnson's grave
Black-and-white photographic portraits by Leary Co.
archival_fonds.historical_significance: Yeigh arranged Johnson's public debut 1891-1892 as Young Men's Liberal Club president

alpine_club_materials.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies (Banff, AB)
alpine_club_materials.extent: 3,084 records, 42 meters textual materials, 12,500 photographs, 85 albums, 8 motion pictures, 3 sound recordings
alpine_club_materials.yeigh_relevance: Charter member; participant 1906 Yoho camp; Adviser Committee 1908-1912
alpine_club_materials.camp_photographs: Official photographs by Byron Harmon (official ACC photographer), Geo. R. B. Kinney, Frank W. Freeborn, and possibly Yeigh
alpine_club_materials.access: Searchable online database with order options for image reproduction

published_works_digitized.title: Through the Heart of Canada
published_works_digitized.subject: Canada - Description and travel (1901-1950)
published_works_digitized.content: Travel narrative across Canada including chapters on Rockies, Selkirks, Kootenays climbing

published_works_digitized.title: Ontario's Parliament Buildings: or, A Century of Legislation, 1792-1892
published_works_digitized.subject: Ontario - Parliament Buildings - History

published_works_digitized.title: The Rainy River District, Province of Ontario, Canada
published_works_digitized.subject: Ontario - Promotional guide - Settlement, mining, timber

primary_source_publications.title: Canada's First Alpine Club Camp
primary_source_publications.author: Frank Yeigh
primary_source_publications.description: First-person narrative of the inaugural 1906 Yoho Pass camp
primary_source_publications.content_summary: Camp life, march to Emerald Lake, delta crossing, Vice-President climb, ceremonies
primary_source_publications.photographs: Official camp photographs by Harmon, Kinney, Freeborn, and Yeigh

related_collections.name: E. Pauline Johnson archives
locations: Six Nations Public Library, Ohsweken, ON
related_collections.relevance: Johnson wrote tribute poem to Kate Westlake Yeigh (Frank's wife); Yeigh organized her public debut

related_collections.name: Canadian Institute / Royal Canadian Institute records
The Globe / Globe and Mail archives
related_collections.relevance: July 1906 dispatches from Yoho camp possibly attributed to Yeigh

research_notes.contemporary_connections: E. Pauline Johnson (cultural readings), A. O. Wheeler (ACC co-founder), Byron Harmon (official photographer)
research_notes.widow: Annie Louise Laird Yeigh - donated fonds to Archives of Ontario March 1959
